items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 27 at 5:46AM EDT by NWS Caribou ME
items.summary:
Roads remain wet, and rapidly falling temperatures behind a cold
front will cause wet roads to freeze, resulting in icy and
slippery roads. Use extra caution across the Route 6, I-95, Route
9 corridor this morning until surfaces are treated or the strong
sun angle helps melt surfaces.
items.area: Central Penobscot; Southern Penobscot; Interior Hancock; Central Washington; Southern Piscataquis; Northern Washington

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 27 at 5:46AM EDT by NWS Caribou ME
items.summary:
Wet untreated surfaces and rapidly falling temperatures froze
overnight resulting in icy and slippery roads. Use extra caution
for this mornings commute with slippery conditions until surfaces
are treated. Air temperatures will remain well below freezing
today and fall midday through the afternoon but the stronger sun
angle will help with melting.
items.area: Northwest Aroostook; Northeast Aroostook; Northern Penobscot; Southeast Aroostook; Central Piscataquis

items.title: Fire Weather Watch issued March 27 at 3:17AM CDT until March 28 at 7:00PM CDT by NWS La Crosse WI
items.summary:
...Fire Weather Watch from 1 PM to 7 PM Saturday for portions of
southeast Minnesota and northeast Iowa...

.Strong winds and very dry air cause potentially critical fire
weather conditions on Saturday afternoon and evening. While near
surface grasses remain green, residual dormant and withered long
grasses could be conducive of ignition and rapid spread.

The National Weather Service in La Crosse has issued a Fire
Weather Watch for strong winds and low humidity, which is in
effect from Saturday
items.area: Mitchell; Howard; Floyd; Chickasaw; Wabasha; Dodge; Olmsted; Mower; Fillmore

items.title: Fire Weather Watch issued March 27 at 3:00AM CDT until March 28 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS Des Moines IA
items.summary:
...Critical Fire Weather Conditions Expected Friday and
Saturday...

.Breezy northwest winds, along with cured fuels and low RH values
in the 20s, especially over southwest to west central Iowa Friday
afternoon to evening will lead to critical fire weather
conditions. Saturday will feature even gustier winds, along with
lower RH values, warming temperatures and dry fuels will lead to
continued critical fire weather conditions. Burning is not
recommended.

The National Weather Service in Des Moin
items.area: Butler; Bremer; Grundy; Black Hawk; Marshall; Tama; Jasper; Poweshiek; Marion; Mahaska; Monroe; Wapello; Appanoose; Davis

items.title: Fire Weather Watch issued March 27 at 2:18AM CDT until March 28 at 7:00PM CDT by NWS Jackson MS
items.summary:
...CRITICAL FIRE WEATHER CONDITIONS POSSIBLE SATURDAY...

* WIND...Northeast 10 TO 20 mph with gusts of up to 30 mph.

* HUMIDITY...As low as 20 percent.

* IMPACTS...The combination of a dry airmass and windy
conditions will result in critical fire weather conditions.
Thus, any fires that develop will likely spread rapidly.
Outdoor burning is not recommended.
